description: Internal sales performance dashboard — call volume, show rates, qualification, lead source attribution, compliance tracking, funnel economics

project_overview.what: The QS Revenue Dashboard pulls sales data from GoHighLevel (CRM) and turns it into a real-time performance dashboard. It tracks call volume, show rates, close rates, lead source attribution, rep-level performance, compliance, and funnel economics (RORI per rep once financial data is entered).
project_overview.why: Lloyd needs to see exactly how each sales rep is performing, which lead sources are producing revenue, and where the pipeline is leaking — without manually pulling GHL reports. This is the operational nervous system for the sales org.
project_overview.end_state: A comprehensive revenue intelligence platform with real-time KPIs, financial integration (Xero for actual revenue vs pipeline), automated anomaly detection, and API endpoints that feed the OpenClaw agent layer for cross-app synthesis.
project_overview.current_summary: Dashboard is live and in daily use across 5 tabs: Sales Performance, Lead Quality by Channel, Pipeline Intelligence, Funnel Economics, and Compliance. Xero invoice sync LIVE — Contract Value on P&L page updates monthly from actual ACCREC invoices with EUR→USD conversion. RORI columns display but require manual period financial inputs (Phase 2 will auto-pull from Xero). Show rate, compliance tracking, and lead quality distribution all working. Lloyd has real-time visibility into rep performance and pipeline economics.

resume_here: DEALS v1 SHIPPED + VERIFIED LIVE (2026-06-14, commit 9977ae2). Live Whop relocated to Deals page, Historical/Live toggle, deal-driven month picker (2026-06 default, reaches May/June). Acceptance criteria verified: month-picker 40-month span, 3 rep buckets, cash/contract/Splitit labels render, P&L Live section removed. Rep fallback (sync_engine Call-2 assignedUserId) correct. Recurring-cash v2 fully spec'd (monthly net + decay model). NEXT: Deploy Deals v1, then recurring-cash Step 2 /ux-design.

current_phase.name: Phase 1 - Live & Iterating
current_phase.goal: Full internal dashboard for Lloyd to monitor sales team performance: call volume per rep, show rates, qualification breakdowns, lead source attribution, compliance tracking, funnel economics with RORI
current_focus: DEALS v1 SHIPPED (2026-06-14): Live Whop on Deals page, P&L cleaned, month picker working
Recurring cash workstream ready: Step 1 verified + locked, Step 2 (monthly net per-rep) ready for /ux-design
immediate_next_steps: Deploy Deals v1 to Railway (git push qs-dashboard subtree)
RECURRING CASH Step 2: /ux-design for monthly-cash backend model (per-month + per-rep granularity), deferred until Deals v1 deployed
RECURRING CASH Step 3-4: FE + build after design locked

known_issues.title: Deals attribution: Unassigned reps due to get_users() name resolution gap
known_issues.status: RESOLVED + VERIFIED 2026-06-11 — fallback deployed, backfill run, deal-level confirmed (Ryan Matsumori 107 deals, April Unassigned 10->2).
known_issues.resolution: get_users() resolves name via firstName+lastName+email fallback (commit be9837d). One-shot backfill POST /api/sync/backfill-attribution (fast/idempotent) applied: 76 users, 3513 opps, 123 deals. Residual ~16 deals genuinely owner-less in GHL (no assignedTo).

known_issues.title: Funnel Economics RORI columns all show — until marketing spend + rep comp are entered
known_issues.resolution: Enter period data via the collapsible Period Financial Inputs panel on the Funnel Economics tab. Data needed: total marketing spend per period, per-rep total comp per period.

known_issues.title: Railway deploy requires manual subtree push — origin push does NOT redeploy qs-dashboard
known_issues.resolution: Deploy cmd: git push qs-dashboard $(git subtree split --prefix=qs-dashboard HEAD):main --force

next_phase.name: No active next phase — dashboard mature
next_phase.goal: Phase 2 (Xero/WAP financial autopull) reconsidered and DROPPED 2026-06-11. The cash-collected half shipped as Whop Live Revenue. The Xero-autopull half is cancelled: Xero is only accurate AFTER human reconciliation (~10-day lag), so auto-pulling unreconciled numbers would feed the dashboard confidently-wrong figures every period — worse than deliberate manual entry. Funnel Economics inputs stay manual, which is correct for a trailing (reconciled-cost) metric. Dashboard is live and mature. Phase 3 (advanced analytics/trends) is the next real candidate once enough synced history accrues.
next_phase.prerequisites: Phase 3 needs 4-6 weeks of synced data for meaningful trends.
deferred_items: Xero financial autopull — CANCELLED (unreconciled Xero not trustworthy for a live dashboard; see decision_log)

future_phases.name: Phase 3 - Advanced Analytics
future_phases.goal: Deeper metric slicing, predictive insights, stronger management views, trend analysis
future_phases.prerequisites: Enough historical data for trends to be meaningful (4-6 weeks of synced data minimum).

recent_milestones.description: DEALS v1 UX relocate shipped (2026-06-14, commit 9977ae2). Live Whop moved from P&L to Deals page with Historical/Live toggle and deal-driven month picker (decoupled from P&L revenue periods, defaults to current month). Fixes critical UX flaw where May/June Whop deals were unreachable. Rep fallback deployed (unassigned opportunity → Call-2 assignee). Pure frontend relocation — no schema/API changes. All 6 acceptance criteria verified. Lloyd can now browse recent deal activity + cash-in alongside historical reconciled deals in one place.

One-shot rep-attribution backfill endpoint shipped (2026-06-13, commit f1de326). POST /api/sync/trigger?sync_type=full now available to backfill all deals at once. Lloyd can run this once to resolve April Unassigned bucket completely. Combined with the get_users() fallback (2026-06-12), P&L now shows accurate rep attribution. Dashboard is mature and live for daily use.

Deal attribution root-cause fixed (2026-06-12, commit be9837d). get_users() now resolves empty `name` fields in GHL /users responses via firstName+lastName+email fallback. This unlocks proper rep name resolution for all ~40% of April deals that were grouped as Unassigned due to missing names in GHL user records. Once full-sync runs, the P&L Unassigned bucket will collapse and reps get proper attribution. The fix is minimal and low-regression: fallback only fires when name is empty, so existing working reps (e.g., Melissa) remain untouched.

Manual whop-refresh endpoint shipped (2026-06-12, commit 10ae80f). POST /pnl/whop-refresh gives Lloyd a one-click refresh-now button for the Whop cash-in data, plus provides a curl-verifiable surface for the EOD batch job (useful for troubleshooting sync failures). Dashboard now has both scheduled syncs and manual refresh capability.

Whop Live Revenue shipped (2026-06-11, commit 15da8da) — per-rep net cash + contract value columns now on the P&L tab. Lloyd can see exactly what cash came in from each rep's closed deals vs. what was forecasted. Paired with Xero invoice sync (2026-06-10) which auto-pulls historical contract value from actual invoices. Dashboard now shows the full cash-flow picture: pipeline (GHL) + signed/invoiced value (Xero) + cash in (Whop). The two feeds (Xero historical contracts + Whop current cash) together give complete visibility into deal lifecycle from close to cash.

Xero invoice sync shipped and LIVE on Railway (2026-06-10). POST /xero/sync-invoices running in production. Contract Value on P&L auto-updates monthly from actual ACCREC invoices. EUR→USD conversion automatic. Category-scoped delete ensures re-runs never touch cash_collected rows.

Show rate numerator bug fixed across all 5 query files — compliance flags (outcome_unfilled, rep_compliance_failure) no longer gate the show count. showed_1st_call_expr() is now the sole show signal, correctly counting stage-based shows where rep moved stage without updating appointment status field.
Lead quality distribution columns added to Rep Breakdown table — Great / Ok / Barely Pass. / Bad / Missing counts visible per rep on Sales Performance tab
Pipeline Intelligence segment-by expanded with 4 new dimensions: Business Fit, Pain/Goal, Industry, Current Revenue — all sourced from existing GHL custom fields
Hover tooltips on every KPI label and column header across all 5 tabs — shows definition and formula on hover using fixed-position JS tooltip (bypasses overflow:hidden clipping)

Funnel Economics tab built — RORI per rep (Contract RORI + Cash RORI), period financial inputs panel (marketing spend + per-rep comp), cost-per-show/qual/close columns. New DB tables: period_marketing_spend, rep_compensation. All columns show — until data entered.
Date picker split into two independent From/To fields — removed flatpickr dependency
Weekly activity view now lets you browse previous weeks — see day-by-day bookings, shows, and qualifications for any week
Rep performance numbers are now clickable — see the specific deals behind each rep's bookings, shows, and closes, with direct links to GoHighLevel
Compliance tracking is live — see which reps aren't logging outcomes, which ones are writing low-quality notes, and disqualification rates per rep
Dashboard launched — key metrics, show rate trends, sales funnel visualization, and per-rep performance table all visible at a glance
First complete data sync from GoHighLevel — pulled in 3,243 deals with zero errors

decision_log.decision: Phase 2 (Xero financial autopull) CANCELLED — Funnel Economics inputs remain manual. v1 DEALS REWORK LOCKED: relocate Live Whop to Deals page (2-lens toggle: Live default, Historical reconciled), rep fallback + Cash/Contract labels, month picker keyed to Whop data not P&L (fixes May/June unreachable).
decision_log.reasoning: Xero data is only accurate AFTER human reconciliation (~10-day lag post-month-close). Auto-pulling unreconciled numbers would feed the dashboard confidently-wrong figures every period — worse than transparent manual entry. The cash-collected half shipped as Whop Live Revenue (verified 2026-06-11). The spend/comp half is NOT a build — it's a data-accuracy gate. Funnel Economics is correctly a trailing/reconciled metric. v1 Deals redesign ships first (front-loads user-facing UX-fixes + unlocks May/June visibility); v2 monthly cash ledger (net-new vs recurring with recurring-decay model) fully spec'd in blueprints/monthly_cash_ledger_blueprint_v2.md but deferred. Mockup approved 2026-06-13.

decision_log.decision: Never auto-pull financials from Xero into the dashboard. Real-time inputs come from operational systems (Whop for cash, ad platforms for spend); Xero is reconciled-only.
decision_log.reasoning: Xero numbers are not accurate until a human reconciles them (~10-day lag) — the same lag that motivated the Whop Live Revenue feature. Auto-pulling unreconciled Xero would feed the dashboard confidently-wrong figures every period, which is worse than deliberate manual entry. This kills the originally-planned Phase 2 'Xero autopull'. RORI is a trailing metric (needs accurate costs), so its manual reconciled inputs are appropriate and stay manual. Live/now metrics (cash collected) use operational sources; trailing/reconciled metrics (RORI) tolerate the lag.

decision_log.decision: Whop processor detection is PAYMENT-level, not membership-level; net cash = total_paid x 0.85 for Splitit/ClarityPay, total_paid otherwise; internal-plan length from split_pay_required_payments
decision_log.reasoning: Verified against live Whop API 2026-06-11. ClarityPay is invisible on the membership (reads 'multi_psp'); only payment.payment_processor exposes 'claritypay'. Financed deals (Splitit/ClarityPay) settle 100% upfront as a single payment, so total_paid x 0.85 is correct from day one. Internal plans only record collected installments, so len(payments) under-counts — split_pay_required_payments is the authoritative plan length. Full model in knowledge/whop_payment_model.md.

decision_log.decision: Xero invoices are the authoritative source for contract value (not GHL projected_deal_size)
decision_log.reasoning: GHL projected_deal_size is unreliable — reps don't fill it in consistently. Xero ACCREC invoices reflect what was actually billed to clients.

decision_log.decision: Contract value stored as one aggregate row per month in revenue_line_items (source=xero, category=contract_value, product_type=invoiced_total)
decision_log.reasoning: Monthly aggregate is sufficient for RORI calculation. Per-deal invoice matching deferred to Phase 3 — adds fuzzy name matching complexity without changing the RORI formula.

decision_log.decision: Category-scoped DELETE before invoice upsert (not replace=True in upsert_revenue_line_items)
decision_log.reasoning: replace=True would wipe cash_collected rows for the same period. Category-scoped delete is surgical — only removes contract_value rows, leaving other categories intact.

decision_log.decision: Shared Xero helpers extracted to api/utils/xero_utils.py instead of xero_auth.py
decision_log.reasoning: xero_auth.py is already 1,107 lines. Adding to it risks further growth and creates router→router import coupling. New shared utils file is the clean boundary.

decision_log.decision: Show count numerator uses showed_1st_call_expr() alone — no compliance flag filter
decision_log.reasoning: compliance flags (outcome_unfilled, rep_compliance_failure) must never gate metric counts. A rep moving to the 'Call 1 Done' stage counts as a show regardless of whether appointment status field was also updated. Compliance flags are for separate compliance tab only.

decision_log.decision: RORI defined as Contract Value ÷ (Rep Comp + Mktg Alloc) for Contract RORI, Cash Collected ÷ (Rep Comp + Mktg Alloc) for Cash RORI
decision_log.reasoning: Marketing allocation per rep = (Total Mktg Spend ÷ Team Total Shows) × Rep Shows. This proportionally assigns shared marketing cost based on each rep's share of team volume.

decision_log.decision: Funnel Economics financial inputs are manually entered (not auto-pulled from Xero yet)
decision_log.reasoning: Xero/WAP API integration is Phase 2. Phase 1 validates the metric logic and UI with manual entry first. Once accounting data is confirmed correct, Phase 2 automates the feed.

decision_log.decision: qs-dashboard lives in monorepo locally but deploys from standalone qs-revenue-dashboard GitHub repo
decision_log.reasoning: Railway service was originally created from the standalone repo. Easiest to maintain — use git subtree push to keep qs-revenue-dashboard in sync with monorepo changes.

decision_log.decision: Sync runs hourly (incremental) with daily Fireflies appointment resolver at 7pm EST
decision_log.reasoning: Hourly incremental keeps data at most ~1.5h stale. Fireflies resolver auto-flips appointment statuses after business hours when all transcripts are ready. Weekly full sync on Sundays catches anything missed.

decision_log.decision: Show/no-show detection via union of appointment status + pipeline stage signals
decision_log.reasoning: No single GHL field reliably indicates show/no-show. Union of signals catches more cases than either alone.

decision_log.decision: Bearer token auth on all API endpoints
decision_log.reasoning: Simple, sufficient for internal tool. Upgrade to JWT/API key scoping when cross-app orchestration needs differentiated access.

accounting_data_needed.note: Required to activate Funnel Economics RORI columns. Collect from accounting call.
items: Total marketing spend per period (from Xero/bookkeeper)
Per-rep total compensation per period (base + commission + bonuses)
Actual signed contract value per closed deal (should be in GHL monetary_value — verify reps are filling it in)
Cash collected per closed deal (from WAP/Stripe refund reports)
Optional: refunds/chargebacks per period to adjust effective cash collected

tech_stack.stripe: Read-only charge/customer lookups only — no webhooks, no writes
tech_stack.integrations: GHL (deal sync), Xero (invoice matching), Stripe (revenue), Wise (payouts), Whop
tech_stack.shared_repo: Geri also pushes to lloyd-yip/qs-revenue-dashboard — NEVER force-push

hard_constraints: Routes → Services/DB. No direct DB calls in route handlers.
Stripe is READ-ONLY. No webhook handler. No write operations. Never add without signature verification.
NEVER force-push to qs-dashboard remote. Always git fetch before pushing. If rejected: fetch and merge.
Bearer token auth on all endpoints.
All OAuth secrets and API keys in environment variables only.
Alembic migrations must be reversible — write down migration before running up.

feature_backlog.title: Relocate Live Whop view from P&L to Deals page
feature_backlog.problem: The live per-rep Whop cash view is buried at the bottom of the P&L (money-summary) page. To see live deal-level activity Lloyd has to open P&L, when it belongs alongside the historical deal reconciliation on the Deals page. CRITICAL UX FLAW (2026-06-11): the Live Whop section is chained to the P&L period dropdown, which is populated from months with P&L data loaded (revenue_line_items/expenses), NOT from months with Whop deals. The dropdown stops at Apr 2026; May/June Whop deals exist in the DB but are UNREACHABLE, and the live view cannot show the current month — undercutting its real-time purpose.
feature_backlog.why_important: P&L is the financial summary; Deals is where deal-level detail lives. Splitting live deals onto P&L makes the information architecture confusing and the live view hard to find. Lloyd explicitly requested the move (2026-06-11).
feature_backlog.user_story: When I open the Deals page, I expect to switch between historical/reconciled deals and live/this-month deals in one place. Currently: reconciled deals are on the Deals page, but live this-month deals are on a separate P&L page.
feature_backlog.success_definition: The Deals page shows a Historical/Live toggle; the Live lens renders the per-rep collapsible Whop view (net cash, contract, fee + confidence badges); the P&L page no longer contains the Live Whop section.
acceptance_criteria: Deals page (static/deals.html) has a visible toggle/tabs switching between 'Historical (Reconciled)' and 'Live (This Month)'
Live lens renders per-rep collapsible buckets with net cash, gross contract, Splitit/ClarityPay -15% badges, HIGH/MEDIUM confidence badges, and an 'as of ... refreshed nightly' line
Live lens shows the correct empty state for a month with no Whop deals (does not error)
P&L page (static/pnl.html) no longer renders #whop-live-section and no longer calls loadWhopLive
GET /api/dashboard/pnl/whop-live still returns 200 and drives the Live lens unchanged

Live lens has its OWN month selector populated from months that have Whop deals (deal_whop_matches.first_payment_date), defaulting to the CURRENT month — decoupled from the P&L revenue-period dropdown so recent/current months (e.g. May/June 2026) are always reachable
A rep's deals are findable without hunting month-by-month — the Live lens makes the active month obvious and/or offers a rep-centric view spanning months (decide in /ux-design)
feature_backlog.general_approach: Pure frontend relocation. Move loadWhopLive/renderWhopLiveSection/renderRepBucket + the Live Whop CSS from pnl.html into deals.html. Add a lens toggle on the Deals page: 'Historical' = existing reconciliation cards/table, 'Live' = the relocated Whop view (calls the existing GET /pnl/whop-live for the current month). Remove the #whop-live-section block + loadWhopLive call from pnl.html. No backend change.
files_in_scope: static/deals.html - add Historical/Live lens toggle; add relocated Live Whop section + its loadWhopLive/render* JS + CSS
static/pnl.html - remove #whop-live-section div, the loadWhopLive call in loadPnL, and the Live Whop CSS/JS
api/routers/whop_live.py - unchanged (endpoint stays; optional future path rename pnl->deals)
constraints: FOCUSED scope - do NOT touch the top navigation in this item
Reuse existing dark-theme design tokens and the existing renderBucket collapsible pattern - no new design system
/ux-design must confirm the Deals-page IA (tabs vs toggle vs stacked) before building
No backend/schema change - GET /pnl/whop-live + POST /pnl/whop-refresh stay as-is
explicitly_out_of_scope: Top-nav consolidation across pages (3 inconsistent navs) - separate backlog item
Renaming the /pnl/whop-live endpoint path to /deals/* - optional, not required
Any change to Whop matching, attribution, or net-cash logic (that work is done)
edge_cases: Current month has zero Whop deals -> Live lens shows 'No Whop-settled deals for this month yet', not an error
Deals page has no month selector today; the Live lens needs a current-month default (and possibly a month picker) distinct from the Historical filter controls
Deals with NULL ghl_owner_name still group under 'Unassigned' in the Live lens (expected)
Switching lenses must not double-fetch or leave stale DOM from the other lens
Current month has Whop deals but no P&L data loaded -> month MUST still appear in the Live lens selector (the bug being fixed)
A rep (e.g. Armando) has deals scattered across months -> appears in each month's lens; no single-month view shows all (the discoverability gap that triggered this)
failure_modes: Live JS moved but its CSS left behind -> badges/buckets render unstyled
pnl.html removal misses the loadWhopLive call -> P&L throws ReferenceError after the JS is deleted
Lens toggle shares element IDs with the reconciliation table -> sort/filter handlers collide
feature_backlog.migration_risk: None - frontend-only, no schema or data change.
feature_backlog.rollback_path: Revert static/deals.html and static/pnl.html to prior versions - instant, no data implications.
